hero.title: Hairspray
hero.reason: In honor of Hairstylist Appreciation Day, Hairspray stands out as a fun and energetic film celebrating the creativity and talents of hairstylists in the 1960s.
hero.blurb: With its catchy musical numbers and uplifting message, Hairspray is a joyful tribute to the power of hair and music to bring people together. The film features an all-star cast and impressive dance choreography.

released.title: Next
released.reason: Released today in 2007, Next is an action-thriller that still holds up today with its unique blend of sci-fi and suspense.
released.blurb: With Nicolas Cage starring as a man who can see into his future, Next offers a thrilling ride full of twists and turns. The film's fast-paced action sequences and intriguing plot make it a compelling watch.

birthday.title: The Devil's Advocate
birthday.reason: Happy birthday to Al Pacino, born April 27, 1940, The Devil's Advocate is a classic film that showcases his incredible acting talents as the charismatic and mysterious John Milton.
birthday.blurb: This supernatural thriller follows a young lawyer who discovers his boss is the devil, and Al Pacino's performance is both captivating and chilling. The film's dark and suspenseful atmosphere makes it a gripping watch.

awful.title: I Know Who Killed Me
awful.reason: a famously bad movie with terrible reviews, I Know Who Killed Me is a convoluted and confusing thriller that fails to deliver on its promising premise.
awful.blurb: With a nonsensical plot and poor acting, this film is a cringeworthy mess that's so bad it's almost good. Lindsay Lohan's performance is particularly disappointing, making this a skippable movie.
